content:

[Weekly BD Report: 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-30]

HURL

- SNAPSHOT OF BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT ACTIVITIES
- [Weekly BD Report 05.05.2026 to 11.05.2026]
- Exploring Opportunity in Data Centre Business for NTPC ................................ ......... 12
- Exploring Opportunity in participation Coal Exchange for NTPC ................................ 12
- SNAPSHOT OF BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT ACTIVITIES
- [Weekly BD Report 05.05.2026 to 11.05.2026]
- A. JVs and Subsidiaries
- HURL:
- IPO & Expansion plan:
- NTPC is making efforts to evolve consensus amongst Promoters to bring IPO of HURL and use
- the proceeds from IPO to fund the capacity expansion of HURL . As recorded in HURL Board
- meeting held on 02.03.2026, NTPC and CIL provided consent for IPO while IOCL and
- FCIL/HFCL did not provide consent for IPO and only NTPC provided consent for expansion.
- Share Allotment:
- FCIL & HFCL have not infused the requisite Rs 128.54 Cr for restoring their shareholding to
- 10.99 % in contravention to the guidance provided in the Secretary (Fertilizer) chaired IMC
- meeting dtd 24.09.2025, even after receiving Rs. 130 cr dividend from HURL , citing non-
- availability of clearance from Competent Authority.
- Basis NTPC request, MoP vide letter dated 25.02.2026 has taken up with Department of
- Fertilizers regarding equity infusion by FCIL & HFCL. In the review meeting on 15.04.2026, MoP
- has informed that they will follow up with DoF and has asked NTPC to request other promoters
- also to take up the matter through their respective Ministries.
- JS (Thermal) held a meeting of all promoters ( NTPC, IOC, CIL, FCIL & HFCL) and their
- respective ministries on 28.04.2026 to discuss NTPC’s issues. Deptt of Fertilizer informed that
- they have sought clarification from Cabinet Secretariate regarding jurisdiction of IMC in the
- matter. MoP informed DoF representatives, who were representing HFCL and IFCL, that the
- equity infusion has to be done and that if required issue will be discussed with Cab inet
- Secretary.
- On the issue of IPO & Expansion, MoP has expressed its opinion that in view of the West Asia
- crisis, Fertilizer sector has assumed critical importance, and hence, HURL should go ahead
- with its IPO and expansion. While NTPC has again indicated its consent on both, CIL conveyed
- its consent on IPO but informed that for expansion, only IPO proceeds should be used as their
- equity is tied up elsewhere. IOCL informed that they are not in favour of IPO or expansion and
- instead expressed their interest in acquiring additional stake s in HURL to get management
- control and improve operational efficiency of HURL. MoP advised that in view of the clearance
- for disinvestment from respective ministries of all three lead Promoters, the matter should be
- again discussed in the Board of HURL and activities for HURL IPO may be started parallelly.
- UPL:
- DIPAM vide OM dtd 28.08.2025 has conveyed in -principle approval of AM for disinvestment
- of NTPC’s stake in UPL . Vide mail dtd 06.04.2026, DIPAM has sought status update on the
- same. CP has submitted the reply on 17.04.2026 basis the inputs from BD.
- UPL had published EOI in Apr’25 for disinvestment of 74% stake (Reliance 50% and NTPC
- 24%) but no offers were received. UPL through its Transaction Advisor (M/s Capital Fortunes)
- is presently pursuing with one interested party through bilateral discussions. A non-binding
- offer from the said party was placed before the UPL Board and found commercially unviable;
- UPL, vide letter dated 29.04.2026, has asked the party to sign NDA and commence due
- diligence, expected to be completed within 4 weeks of NDA execution.
- UPL / Transaction Advisor has been verbally informed about NTPC’s interest to disinvest 100%
- stake in the JV .
- NGEL:
- The status for Asset Valuation is as follows:
- o Solar projects of 20 MW or more (except Nokh): Due Diligence is in progress.
- SNAPSHOT OF BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT ACTIVITIES
- [Weekly BD Report 05.05.2026 to 11.05.2026]
- BFNESL:
- Winding up of BFNESL was approved by NTPC in 2017 and by MoP in 2018, subject to
- conditions of appointment of an Independent Liquidator, Compliances with applicable
- regulations and Protection of Government of India’s interest.
- Liquidator appointed in 2018, valuation done by independent valuers in 2019 and 2020. Due
- to high circle rate, liquidation not yet completed. Several meetings held with Liquidator and
- BFNESL committee members from BF and NTPC.
- During the contributories Meeting with liquidator held on 17.04.2026, wherein it was
- discussed to approach Collector for intimation of e -Auction and to carry out revaluation of
- BFNESL land.
- Draft letter to be sent to Collector has been shared by Liquidator on 07.05.2026 and is
- under review by committee members.
- JPL:
- Stake Sale by SFCs:
- Secured Financial Creditors (SFCs) have conveyed their intention to sell their stake in JPL and
- agreed to issue a formal offer to NTPC. SFCs have engaged M/s BDO Valuation Advisory for
- sell-side valuation. It is learnt that Valuation exercise has been completed, and PFC will now
- proceed to take the views of other SFCs in the matter.
- NTPC has taken up valuation of JPL through PwC. The Valuation Model submitted by PwC was
- reviewed and detailed observations were shared . PwC to submit finalised model by
- 11.05.2026.
- Money Laundering Case against Avantha Group by ED:
- ED has filed a Prevention of Corruption Case (PC) against Avantha Group and JPL is one of the
- accused. Next hearing in Trial Court is on 25.05.2026. JPL has filed petition against the trial
- court notice in Delhi High Court, and the matter is listed for hearing on 13.05.2026.
- Arbitration with BHEL:
- Arbitral Tribunal has completed hearing main arguments of BHEL and JPL on the matter.
- Rejoinder arguments of BHEL are to be heard from 23.07.2026 onwards and
- subsequently JPL’s arguments shall be heard.
- NGSL:
- NGSL is considering total dilution up to 26% (13% by each Promoter) in the JV -- NGSL,
- through its Transaction Advisor (TA), M/s E&Y , is in the process of identifying potential
- Investors. NGSL has been asked to provide indicative timelines for the process.
- NGSL vide mail dtd 10.04.2026, has sought guidance and necessary approvals from the
- Promoters on (i) aligning the AoA/MoA with its expanded business scope, and (ii) finalizing and
- ratifying the proposed amendments to the Business Co -operation Agreement ( BCA) with
- GEPIL, in view of certain unresolved issues.
- It is observed that the issues flagged by NGSL regarding AoA/MoA amendment are adequately
- addressed in the SJVA proposed to be signed between NTPC and GE . The draft of the SJVA
- was agreed in Mar 2025 but SJVA could not be signed as BCA amendments are not yet agreed.
- BCA amendment issue has to be resolved between NGSL and GE.
- NESCL:
- NTPC Board approval to grant in -principle consent to NESCL for setting up a Transmission
- System at Great Nicobar Island (GNI) was approved on 26.09.2025.
- The application was filed with JERC on 31.12.2025 and is currently under examination by
- JERC.
- SNAPSHOT OF BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT ACTIVITIES
- [Weekly BD Report 05.05.2026 to 11.05.2026]
- Hearing before JERC was done on 24.02.2026. During hearing, JERC desired that NESCL
- should submit a letter from NTPC Ltd stating that it has nominated NESCL for undertaking the
- work of establishing Transmission system at GNI. A letter has been issued to N ESCL on
- 03.03.2026 for submission at JERC. The letter has been sent to JERC on 12.03.2026.
- The recommendation from the Electricity Department of Andaman and Nicobar was received
- on 28.03.2026, which is one of the requirements prior to the grant of a licence by JERC.
- Hearing held on 05.05.2026 and NESCL petition got admitted.
- NHPTL:
- In the 92nd Governing Council meeting of CPRI dated 27.02.2024, CMD NTPC expressed
- whether the shareholding of NTPC, NHPC and DVC in NHPTL can be taken over by PGCIL and
- CPRI.
- A meeting of Promoter’s representatives was held on 24.02.2025 for proceeding as per JVA
- terms. Accordingly, CPRI, with consent of Promoters, engaged M/s BDO Valuation Advisory on
- 22.09.2025 for fair price valuation of shares of NHPTL.
- Subsequent to meeting held with CPRI, DVC and BDO on 14.11.2025, sharing of draft
- Valuation summary report with Promoters on 06.01.2026 and regular follow -ups on detailed
- report, CPRI on 27.03.2026 shared Valuation report. Revised Draft valuation report received
- on 21.04.2026 and NTPC observations mailed to CPRI on 27.04.2026.
- PVUNL:
- Letter received from PVUNL on 26.12.2025 regarding equity requirement of Rs.325 Cr for Coal
- Mine Development as Stage-I Forest Clearance is under advance stage. Rights issue offer of
- PVUNL awaited. PVUNL has informed you that the proposal shall be sent after approval from
- its Board.
- As informed by PVUNL, at present there is no Director from Govt. Of Jharkhand on PVUNL
- Board, thus the matter couldn’t be put up in the last Board meeting held in March’26.
- Further, on follow-up done on 30.04.2026 with PVUNL, they have informed that appointment
- of Director from Govt. Of Jharkhand may be done in May’26.
- NEWS:
- NTPC and MCD approved the purchase of MCD’s 26% equity stake in NEWS by NTPC at face
- value (Rs.10/share) on 26.09.2025 and 16.02.2026, respectively.
- NTPC, MCD and NEWS signed the Termination Agreement on 07.05.2026 for termination
- of the Joint Venture Agreement and exit of MCD from NEWS through transfer of MCD’s
- 26% equity stake in NEWS to NTPC.
- Follow-up on consultant report on transfer of Waste-to-Wealth assets from NVVN to NEWS,
- CEO, NVVN done on 30.04.2026. Consultant informed inputs provided by N VVN regarding
- Land documents and Arbitration matters related to Bhatinda plant are under study. Meeting
- convened with consultant on 04.05.2026, wherein consultant was asked to expedite
- study related to Bhatinda project and accordingly finalize asset transfer report.
- NSPCL:
- New 800 MW unit at Bhilai : Indian Railways (IR) has conveyed in -principle consent to the
- proposal for JV with NSPCL for new 800 MW unit at Bhilai for captive use (agreed off-take 680
- MW). NTPC has also given in -principal consent on 02.06.2025 to NSPCL. Consent for JV
- formation of NSPCL with IR and land approval awaited from SAIL.
- Subsequent to SAIL consent on 17.10.2025 for DPR preparation of the proposed unit, NSPCL
- awarded the assignment to NTPC Consultancy on 06.11.2025.
- SNAPSHOT OF BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT ACTIVITIES
- [Weekly BD Report 05.05.2026 to 11.05.2026]
- Promoters meet held on 26 December’25. Matters related to new unit of 1X800 MW at NSPCL
- Bhilai, NSPCL’s IPO and fulfilment of RE requirement of SAIL by NGEL discussed. MoM issued
- on 12.01.2026.
- NSPCL has sent a letter to Railways on 16.02.2026 regarding signing of MoU b/w NSPCL and
- Railways for the proposed 1X800 MW unit.
- Final DPR shared with NSPCL by NTPC consultancy on 05.03.2026, to be put up for NSPCL
- Board approval in upcoming Board meeting.
- Construction of ETP:
- NSPCL vide email dated 02.04.2026 has informed SAIL proposal for setting up Effluent
- Treatment Plant (ETP) and utilising water requirement for proposed 800 MW Project. NTPC
- reply to NSPCL proposal sent on 21.04.2026.
- As informed by NSPCL on 01.05.2026, NSPCL has taken up the matter with SAIL. Reply from
- SAIL awaited.
- NSPCL vide email dated 05.05.2026 has informed that NSPCL Board in its meeting held
- on 29.04.2026 has accorded in principle approval for development of 10 MW Floating
- Solar Expansion Project at Maroda Reservoir–I, Bhilai along with approval of DPR and draft
- FAR.
- It is further informed that the 10 MW Floating Solar project will be financed by NSPCL
- through internal resources and no equity contribution from promoters is required.
- THDCIL:
- THDC Khurja Asset Transfer:
- Meeting held under the Chairmanship of Secretary (Power) on 08.12.2025 regarding
- transfer of Khurja STPP and Amelia Coal Mine from THDCIL to Meja Urja Nigam Pvt. Ltd.
- (MUNPL). In the meeting the issue related to the ongoing Supreme Court case filed by the
- State of Uttarakhand against the Union of India in 2012, concerning transfer of THDC
- shareholding to Uttarakhand, was deliberated. ACS(UP) apprised that the issue will be
- discussed internally, and response will be provided within a month. Legal opinion in the
- matter received on 02.02.2026 from Shri Sanjay Jain, Senior Advocate.
- The issue concerning UP’s reply was discussed in the meeting chaired by AS (Thermal) on
- 06.02.2026, wherein it was decided that MoP will convene a meeting with GoUP for further
- discussions in line with the meeting held on 08.12.2026. Meeting which was scheduled
- for 06.05.2026 is again postponed.
- MUNPL:
- Signing of SJVA-III between NTPC and UPRVUNL is presently awaiting final approval from
- the Government of Uttar Pradesh . It is learnt that the proposal for investment approval of
- Meja-II (3×800 MW) along with SJVA -III was placed before the State Expert Appraisal
- Committee of UP for review and that the Committee has recommended the removal of the 50%
- manpower deployment clause from the draft SJVA-III (as modified unilaterally by UPRVUNL).
- The matter was further deliberated in the UP-Energy Task Force (ETF) meeting on 27.04.2026,
- chaired by the Chief Secretary, Uttar Pradesh. The proposal is expected to be placed before the
- UP Cabinet for final approval in due course.
- BRBCL:
- Capacity Expansion: Indian Railways (IR) had initially (21.02.2025) accorded consent for
- expansion units of 2x250 MW at BRBCL. However, based on IR request to optimize Project Cost
- and Power Tariff, NTPC’s consent for project expansion for higher capacity (1X800 MW) sent on
- 09.05.2025.
- Letter dated 23.09.2025 sent to IR to expedite signing of PPA for the proposed 1X800 MW unit.
- SNAPSHOT OF BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT ACTIVITIES
- [Weekly BD Report 05.05.2026 to 11.05.2026]
- IR vide letter dated 23.10.2025 has informed regarding offtake of 90% power from 1X800 MW
- unit of BRBCL.
- Comments from Legal received. Revised draft Supplementary JVA (SJVA) amendment shared
- with BRBCL on 23.02.2026 for getting IR comments before management approval.
- As informed by BRBCL on 27.02.2026, draft pre -feasibility report for the new unit has been
- shared by NTPC Consultancy. BRBCL has informed on 07.05.2026 that at present following
- Pre-feasibility studies are in progress : Watershed Development Study, Ground
- Penetration Report (GPR) Survey and Biodiversity Analysis Study. After finalization of Pre-
- feasibility report, same shall be put up for BRBCL Board approval.
- TELK:
- Discussion held with Principal Secretary, Industries GoK on 12.11.2025 for Exit from TELK.
- MoM of the meeting held on 12.11.2025 issued wherein the way forward for Auditing is
- discussed and GoK has requested NTPC for bearing the cost of consultant for valuation.
- Letter dtd 28.11.2025 received from Principal Secretary – Industries, GoK mentioning that
- proposal to appoint a mutually agreeable consultant for exit of NTPC is acceptable subject to
- MD of TELK being held by a representative of Kerala while the Chairman t o be nominated by
- NTPC Ltd (Presently, MD (Sh Neeraj Mittal) appointed by NTPC and Chairman (Adv P C Joseph)
- from GoK-as per Share Holders Agreement).
- Letter dt. 08.01.2026 sent to TELK for taking up with GoK for appointment of PwC as mutually
- agreed Consultant but no reply received.
- NTECL
- PSP by NTECL: Upper Bhavani 1000 MW PSP has been allotted to NTECL for execution by
- Govt of Tamil Naidu.
- Subsequent to trifurcation of TANGEDCO, Supplementary Joint Venture Agreement (SJVA) is to
- be signed with TANGEDCO successor.
- Meanwhile, TNGECL (Tamil Nadu Green Energy Corporation Limited) has indicated their
- willingness to sign SJVA as third equity partner towards implementation of Upper Bhavani in
- NTECL, without changing the overall equity participation 50:50.
- Letter dtd 07.01.2026 sent to MD -TNGECL requesting them to submit a detailed proposal
- outlining their proposed role along with proposed organizational structure.
- Meeting held with TNGECL on 03.02.2026 with respect to SJVA of Upper Bhavani. TNGECL has
- shared their proposal. Execution of SJVA for NTECL followed up with JV partner vide email
- dated 23.02. 2026.TNGECL vide email dated 09.03.2026, requested NTPC remarks on its
- proposal.
- Matter discussed with CEO(NTECL) on 11.05.2026 for signing of SJVA. CEO(NTECL)
- conveyed that matter will be suitably taken up with Govt. of Tamil Nadu.
- NBPPL:
- Considering NBPPL’s immediate fund requirement of ~ Rs. 130 Cr, NTPC (04.12.2025)
- requested NBPPL to obtain BHEL’s confirmation for release of its proportionate share.
- Accordingly, NBPPL (05.12.2025 and 09.02.2026) sought BHEL’s confirmation.
- BHEL (10.02.2026) highlighted its outstanding receivables from NBPPL and requested NTPC
- to release outstanding payment of Rs. 111 Cr related to Unchahar project to NBPPL. NTPC
- responded on 20.02.2026, BHEL reiterated its position on 23.02.2026. NTPC reply sent on
- 11.03.2026.
- On NBPPL ’s request dated 18.04.2026, NTPC nominated Shri Asutosh Biswas, GM
- (O&M), Unchahar on 05.05.2026 for a committee to review an employee appeal against
- SNAPSHOT OF BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT ACTIVITIES
- [Weekly BD Report 05.05.2026 to 11.05.2026]
- the Disciplinary Authority penalty order regarding billing discrepancies in the Unchahar
- Main Plant Civil Package.
- AMRCD proceedings against BHEL
- Dispute Notice issued to BHEL on 27.11.2025 for unresolved issues of NBPPL under Clause
- 17 (Dispute Resolution and Arbitration) of JVA.
- BHEL (12.12.2025) sought equal sharing of NBPPL liabilities excluding Unchahar, and release
- of outstanding payment of Rs. 111 Cr (Unchahar) by NTPC; NTPC replied on 28.01.2026.
- BHEL (25.02.2026) reiterated its stance citing Unchahar revenues and EPC contract
- amendment; NTPC responded on 11.03.2026.
- NTPC on 23.03.2026 requested MoP to refer the dispute with BHEL to the First Tier of AMRCD.
- During AS(Thermal) review on 15.04.2026, MoP proposed a joint meeting at Ministry level to
- facilitate resolution of the NBPPL issues.
- MoP , vide letter dated 20.04.2026, requested NTPC to explore, once more, additional avenues
- for amicable resolution of the issues with BHEL at appropriate levels. Accordingly, NTPC,
- vide letter dated 05.05.2026, requested BHEL for an Apex-level meeting.
- In a meeting held on 29.04.2026, under the chairmanship of JS (Thermal), NTPC made a
- presentation on issues related to NBPPL . JS (Thermal) advised NTPC and BHEL to (i) once
- again explore possibilities of engagement of NBPPL employees, (ii) take up infusion of funds
- (Rs. 130 Cr) in NB PPL as immediate financial assistance, and (iii) reconcile and settle
- liabilities of NBPPL. Draft Board Note for approval for infusion of Rs. 65 Cr in NBPPL is
- prepared and under review.
- Delhi High Court (HC) Case: NBPPL vs BTL EPC Ltd.
- BTL filed execution petition; HC (28.07.2025) directed NBPPL to deposit Rs. 17.83 Cr or file
- assets list by 02.09.2025. NBPPL filed list of assets on 08.11.2025.
- BTL (15.10.2025) sought impleadment of shareholders; NTPC filed reply on 09.12.2025.
- Matter heard on 24.03.2026 by Hon’ble HC Justice Sh. Harish Vaidyanathan Shankar. BTL
- contended that corporate veil of NBPPL should be lifted in view of its weak financial position.
- However, the Court was not persuaded, observing that no legal basis had be en furnished in
- support of the request. The Court directed that all applications in the matter shall be taken up
- on the next date of hearing, i.e., 06.08.2026.
- EESL:
- Debt Condition
- EESL is in financial distress due to large outstanding dues of Rs. 3830 Cr., excluding Delayed
- Payment Surcharge. as on 28.02.2026. EESL has managed to service its debt obligations till
- Feb’26.
- Divestment of stake in M/s Intellismart (IIPL)
- Pursuant to approval in EESL Board, in its 174th meeting on 03.12.2025, regarding joint stake
- sale in IIPL. NIIF has engaged M/s Deloitte for initiating stake sale process, M/s PwC for financial
- and commercial due diligence, and M/s TT&A for legal due diligence. Non -Disclosure
- Agreements (NDAs) were executed with prospective investors (PIs) and the Information
- Memorandum, along with the Financial Model, has been shared.
- Non-binding offers have been received from the PIs and are currently under assessment.
- Impairment Testing
- As per requirement of NTPC Finance by email dated 11.03.2026 requested Impairment testing
- of EESL through external agency by 31.03.2026 and subsequent approval obtained, M/s
- Proxcel was engaged for impairment testing of EESL and draft report has been received on
- 30.03.2026.
- Final report submitted by M/s Proxcel on 25.04.2026, with 69.8 cr impairment
- SNAPSHOT OF BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT ACTIVITIES
- [Weekly BD Report 05.05.2026 to 11.05.2026]
- ASHVINI:
- On 15.10.2025, the Department of Atomic Energy (DAE) granted approval for transfer of
- MBRAPP project from NPCIL to ASHVINI at book value.
- ASHVINI Board approved the draft ATA in its meeting held on 06.01.2026 and the ATA signed
- on 12.01.2026. Accordingly, consideration amount of Rs. 766.25 Cr has been transferred by
- ASHVINI to NPCIL on 12.02.2026.
- Upon receipt of land NOC from NPCIL, ASHVINI applied on 18.02.2026 to the Revenue
- Department (through the District Collector) for change of land title registration.
- ASHVINI, vide letter dated 14.01.2026, requested NTPC for equity release of Rs. 392 Cr
- through Rights Offer, which was released on 29.01.2026 after approval of the Competent
- Authority.
- B. Asset Monetization
- Targets for FY’25 to FY’30 as communicated by MoP to NTPC as per the table given below:
- (In Rs. Crores)
- PSU 2025-
- 26
- 2026-
- 27
- 2027-28 2028-
- 29
- 2029-30 Total Remarks
- NTPC - 3,000
- (Listing
- of
- HURL
- and
- Exit of
- NTPC)
- 9,000
- (NGEL
- follow
- on
- Public
- Offer)
- 15,000
- (NGEL
- follow
- on
- Public
- Offer)
- 27,000 Actual realization during
- follow-on will depend on
- the prevailing market
- conditions.
- THDC - - - 1,000 - 1,000 Listing of THDC
- NEEPCO - - - 1,000 - 1,000 Listing of NEEPCO
- C. Other Avenues
- Exploring Partnership with GMDC & GUVNL:
- Post signing of MoUs with GMDC for coal supply and coal/lignite gasification, a draft FSA was
- shared with GMDC on 19.03.2026 for review, response awaited.
- A draft MoU was shared with GUVNL on 04.02.2026 with incorporation of cost of power, based on
- GMDC provided ex-mine coal price. GUVNL has sought clarifications on certain points vide email
- dated 16.03.2026. Inputs from NTPC Engineering team for submission of the reply to GUVNL are
- awaited.
- Exploring Opportunities in Himachal Pradesh State Power Distribution Sector:
- Business opportunities in the Himachal Pradesh state power distribution sector are currently
- being explored with a focus on improving the States power distribution infrastructure.
- Meeting with the Chairman (HPSEBL) held on 17.12.2025, HPSEBL requested NTPC to install
- Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) at its substations at Baddi industrial area. Accordingly,
- site visit was done at three substations in Baddi, Himachal Pradesh, for preliminary assessment
- of BESS on 17.12.2025.
- Letter received from HPSEBL on 25.02.2026 wherein they have asked for further data to enable
- approval of the proposal. Reply sent to HPSEBL with revised pre-feasibility report on 13.03.2026.
- The meeting with the HPSEBL Director (Tech) held on 31.03.2026 to discuss and expediated the
- SNAPSHOT OF BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT ACTIVITIES
- [Weekly BD Report 05.05.2026 to 11.05.2026]
- NTPC proposal. Letter from Chief Engineer, HPSEBL, for detailed discussions received as per
- email dated 28.04.2026, sent to NGEL.
- Meeting done with Chief Engineer on 11.05.2026 to brief the study undertaken by NGEL and
- the subsequent proposal.
- Exploring Opportunities in BIHAR State Power Distribution Sector:
- Business opportunities in the Bihar state power distribution sector are currently being explored
- with a focus on improving the States power distribution infrastructure.
- A meeting with the Directors of NBPDCL and SBPDCL, along with other senior officials, was held
- on 23/24 December, and the MoM received on 29.12.2025.
- SBPDCL and NBPDCL will review NTPC’s proposal and convey them for further action within 2
- weeks. A reminder email has been sent to NBPDCL and SBPDCL on 16.01.2026,09.02.2026.
- As discussed with the DISCOM officials on 11.03.2026 and 13.03.2026 , the approval file for
- further actions at SBPDCL is under approval, and the reply from NBPDCL is still awaited
- In the meeting held on 04.05.2026 between CMD sir and Secretary ( Energy) Bihar, the issue of
- approvals for DISCOM works was discussed. Secretary (Energy) assured that the approvals will be
- accorded shortly regarding construction of new sub stations and renovation of existing sub stations.
- In the meeting it was discussed to execute the work on priority, the same has also been conveyed
- to the Consultancy Group on 04.05.2026. Reply from DISCOMs awaited.
- Potential Investment in M/s Delectrik Systems Pvt Ltd:
- Proposal for potential investment in M/s Delectrik Systems Pvt Ltd through BD budget approved
- in-principle by NTPC Board.
- PwC has been appointed as consultant for valuation and due diligence. Management guidance has
- been received during review meetings on 02.03.2026, 31.03.2026 and 06.04.2026.
- Due-diligence Committee report approved
- POSC approval note initiated on 09.05.2026 and is under approval.
- Exploring Opportunities in Nuclear Energy Business:
- Joint Venture with Assystem International, France:
- Signing of MoU is pending due to clearance awaited from DAE & MEA since October 2025. Nuclear
- cell is following up the matter.
- Draft JVA for Engineering and Project Management services in Nuclear Energy is under discussion.
- In an internal meeting held on 05.03.2026 , the JVA was reviewed in line with the Apex level
- decision on 26.02.2026 regarding revised shareholding pattern (NTPC-49%, Assystem-51%). The
- draft was finalized internally on 11.03.2026 and the r evised version was shared by nuclear cell
- with Assystem on 12.03.2026.
- Upon receipt of comments from Assystem on 23.03.2026, an ED level meeting was held on
- 27.03.2026 to resolve the issues raised by Assystem.
- Meetings held with Assystem on 01.04.2026 and 07.04.2026 to finalize the draft JVA. Clauses
- relating to ROFO, quorum for Board and General Meetings, Functional Management, and Reserved
- Matters remain under discussion.
- Joint Venture with L&T, India:
- MoU signed with L&T on 13.06.2025 for collaboration in Nuclear Energy Business.
- A Joint Working Group of NTPC’s Nuclear Cell and L&T have prepared a draft Joint Venture
- Agreement (JVA) for setting up nuclear power plants in India.
- Nuclear Cell has initiated Board Note for approval of the draft JVA on 08.12.2025.
- Draft JVA revised and finalized on 20.12.2025 based on c omments received from Legal, CS and
- Finance. Revised draft JVA shared with L&T. L&T response awaited.
- Nuclear Cell informed on 30.03.2026 that despite repeated follow-ups, L&T has not responded
- regarding the JVA.
- SNAPSHOT OF BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT ACTIVITIES
- [Weekly BD Report 05.05.2026 to 11.05.2026]
- Joint Venture with Holtec, USA:
- MoU signed with Holtec on 19.01.2026 for collaboration in Nuclear Energy Business.
- A Joint Working Group of NTPC’s Nuclear Cell and Holtec have prepared a draft Joint Venture
- Agreement (JVA) for setting up nuclear power plants in India.
- Draft JVA under discussion. NTPC comments / observations obtained from all concerned
- departments; to be shared by Nuclear Cell to Holtec.
- MoU with TENEX, a subsidiary of Atomic Energy Power Corporation, Rosatom, Russia:
- Draft MoU for assessment of Uranium assets opportunities and nuclear fuel cycle products &
- services opportunities is under discussion with TENEX. NTPC comments / observations shared
- with TENEX on 11.02.2026 and TENEX comments / observations received on 19.02.2026.
- NTPC’s consolidated comments on the revised draft shared with TENEX on 03.03.2026.
- MoU with EDF, France:
- NTPC Competent Authority (on 13.11.2025) accorded approval for signing MoU with EDF for
- collaboration in nuclear energy business.
- Nuclear Cell approached MoP (on 18.11.2025) for getting approval of DAE & MEA, for signing of the
- MoU. A Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) was signed between NTPC and EDF on 19.12.2025.
- Email dated 06.04.2026 conveyed MoP’s letter dated 27.03.2026, communicating the approval of
- the Hon’ble Minister of Power —subsequent to concurrence from DAE and MEA —for NTPC’s
- proposal to engage with EDF , France through a non-binding MoU, which was subsequently signed
- on 08.04.2026.
- Exploring utilization of Gas-based IC Engines for flexible operations:
- Finland Embassy, vide letter dtd. 17.02.2025 to Secretary (Power), India has submitted a proposal
- of Wartsila, Finland for a 300 MW pilot power project with NTPC under the G2G arrangement.
- Wartsila shared a draft MoU with NTPC on 15.05.2025 for setting up a 300 MW gas engine-based
- pilot power project at NTPC’s Anta power plant site . NTPC comments shared with Wartsila on
- 14.07.2025, and Wartsila position received on 13.08.2025.
- Subsequently, a meeting with Wartsila held on 10.03.2026 and pending issues discussed.
- Accordingly, a revised draft MoU is shared on 13.03.2026 with concerned departments
- (Engineering, Legal, DRC and Finance) for their review. The finalized version is shared with M/s
- Wartsila on 17.03.2026.
- Vide email dated 30.03.2026, Wartsila raised concerns on some of the clauses. The issues were
- discussed in a meeting between NTPC (representatives of BD, Engg. & Finance) and Wartsila on
- 17.04.2026. As deliberated in the meeting, inputs from NTPC Engineering were submitted to
- Wartsila on 09.05.2026. A revised version will be shared by Wartsila for NTPC review.
- JVA with EDF Power Solutions India Pvt Ltd.:
- NTPC Board in its 562nd meeting held on 23.12. 2025 approved the proposal for formation of JV
- with EDF Power Solutions India Pvt Ltd. for development of PSP on JV basis and incorporation of
- 50:50 JV company. EDF Power Solutions India Pvt Ltd Board extracts received on 05.02.2026.
- BD sent a letter to MoP for approval of DIPAM on 06.02.2026.
- MoP forwarded the request on 25.03.2026 to DIPAM for approval of JVC. Telecom discussion held
- with Director DIPAM on 15.04.2026 with respect to subject proposal. DIPAM has requested for
- clarifications with respect to subject matter vide email dtd 27.04.2026. Reply sent to DIPAM on
- 11.05.2026.
- Exploring opportunities with SUEZ India Pvt Ltd.:
- SUEZ India provides integrated water and wastewater solutions, including treatment plants,
- distribution management, and long-term O&M for municipal and industrial clients.
- Based on discussions, following potential avenues carrying synergies were identified:
- o NTPC Plants water optimisation.
- SNAPSHOT OF BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT ACTIVITIES
- [Weekly BD Report 05.05.2026 to 11.05.2026]
- o Waste-to-Energy
- o Water desalination / management for Green Chemical Hub.
- o Sludge Management
- As per the mutually discussed broad contours for collaboration during the meeting on 08.04.2026,
- SUEZ has shared a short proposal on NTPC plant water optimisation on 23.04.2026. The same
- has been shared with CC -OS for evaluation of potential engagement opp ortunity. Also, BD team
- has advised SUEZ to engage with NGEL for Desalination Plant at Pudimadaka. Additionally, SUEZ
- has been advised to explore business models and opportunities in WtE and sludge management.
- Exploring opportunities in Combined Heat and Power (CHP) Business:
- During NTPC Strategy Meet (Jan’26), it was suggested to explore opportunities for catering to
- industrial heat load in captive mode.
- To develop deeper insights into the prospects and strategic fit of CHP for NTPC, PwC has been
- engaged as consultant on 03.04.2026 to carry out a broad assessment under its current
- assignment with Corporate Planning on retainership basis . PwC share d preliminary industry
- insights on 1 3.04.2026. Follow-up review meeting on the progress of the assignment is
- planned on 14.05.2026.
- Exploring Documentation of Startup policy for NTPC limited
- During NTPC’s Strategic Meet 2026 held on 01.01.2026, deliberations were done on exploring
- investments in future-oriented businesses and supporting startups through the creation of a
- Startup Fund, with a view to deriving both financial and branding advantages for NTPC.
- BD has studied the startup policies and practices adopted by peer PSUs in India and the matter
- was deliberated to Management.
- Further, in principle approval for developing Comprehensive Startup Policy for NTPC limited and
- appointment of consultant obtained from competent authority.
- Note for technical and administrative approval for engagement of consultant for developing
- startup policy, has been approved by Competent authority on 06.05.2026. File forwarded to
- CC&M for further processing.
- Exploring Opportunity in Data Centre Business for NTPC
- Vide email dtd 15.04.2026, Corp. Planning (CP) shared a study report by M/s AECOM on feasibility
- of NTPC venturing into Data Centre business at Simhadri location.
- On 16.04.2026, a meeting was held with CP wherein NTPC’s prospects for venturing into the Data
- Centre business were discussed. CP subsequently shared a draft Scope of Work for review.
- Based on internal review, BD has shared certain changes in the draft scope of work with CP .
- CP has sought budgetary quotes from agencies on the proposed scope of work. Quotes from
- AECOM, KPMG and Deloitte have been received till 08.05.202 6 while that from PwC is
- expected by 11.05.2026.
- Exploring Opportunity in participation Coal Exchange for NTPC
- Vide discussions with National Stock Exchange of India on 30.4.2026, it is gathered that NSE is in
- the process of setting up a coal exchange under the name “National Coal Exchange of India” , with
- incorporation targeted by May 2026.
- Internal d iscussions are in pro gress regarding NTPC’s proposed participation in the coal
- exchange, including indicative equity stake (~5%).
